RESPONSE TO PLTF'S OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE FILED BY DEFENDANT BINGHAM MCCUTCHEN, LLP, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY PARTNERSHIP HINMAN, FRANK AN INDIVIDUAL ABRAMS, WILLIAM AN INDIVIDUAL

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS BINGHAM MCCUTCHEN, LLP, FRANK HINMAN, WILLIAM ABRAMS AMENDED NOTICE OF HEARING ON DEFENDANTS' PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE OVERRULED. WRITTEN AGREEMENT CONTAINS VALID AND BINDING ARBITRATION CLAUSE, AND APPLIES TO "ANY DISPUTE OR CLAIMS." PLAINTIFF FAILS TO PRESENT EVIDENCE THAT THE AGREEMENT IS UNCONSCIONABLE OR FRAUDULENTLY INDUCED. BUSINESS AND PROFESSIONS CODE SECTION 6148 NOT APPLICABLE DUE TO EMERGENCY NATURE OF SERVICES REQUIRED AND LLC STATUS OF CLIENT. THE AGREEMENT DATED APRIL 23, 2007 PUT PLAINTIFF ON NOTICE THAT IT WAS FOR A NEW MATTER AND WAS FOR AN ENGAGEMENT TO REPRESENT PLAINTIFF TO OVERTURN THE PARTIAL SUMMARY JUDGMENT "AND ANY OTHER ISSUES AS MAY ARISE IN THE LITIGATION." (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: HELEN SOREP-MATLEY, CSR 	225

Amended Notice Of Hearing On Defts' Petition To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Mar-12-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS BINGHAM MCCUTCHEN, LLP, FRANK HINMAN, WILLIAM ABRAMS amended Notice of Hearing on Defendants' Petition to Compel Arbitration. Appearances: Karl I. Mullen, Esq. of Mullen Law Firm PC (503) 297-4594 appearing for Plaintiffs and opposing party; Pamela Phillips Esq. & Amy L. Bomse, Esq. of Howard Rice (415) 677-6590 appearing for Defendants and Moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Petition to compel arbitration is granted. Plaintiff's objections to evidence overruled. Written agreement contains valid and binding arbitration clause, and applies to "any dispute or claims." Plaintiff fails to present evidence that the agreement is unconscionable or fraudulently induced. Business and Professions Code section 6148 not applicable due to emergency nature of services required and LLC status of client. The agreement dated April 23, 2007 put plaintiff on notice that it was for a new matter and was for an engagement to represent plaintiff to overturn the partial summary judgment "and any other issues as may arise in the litigation." (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Helen Sorep-Matley CSR # 9225

Notice Of Defendants' Petition To Compel Arbitration; Declarations Of Frank M. Hinman And Pamela Phillips In Support
Off Calendar Mar-11-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT BINGHAM MCCUTCHEN, LLP, FRANK HINMAN, and, WILLIAM ABRAMS Notice Of Defendants' Petition to Compel Arbitration. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Matter is continued to March 12, 2010 per amended notice of motion. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
